channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump floats reducing tariffs on China to secure TikTok deal
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday floated reducing tariffs on China to get a TikTok divestiture deal done as the April 5 deadline nears.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Misinformation expert encouraged picket lines at Tesla sites to protest DOGE cuts. Then came the online threats
item.description: (CNN) — Sociologist Joan Donovan rarely looks at social media posts about herself on X anymore. She already knows what’s being said.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Red Hat’s AI portfolio evolves to address enterprise deployment challenges
item.description: Red Hat announced updates to Red Hat OpenShift AI and Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI (RHEL AI), with a goal of addressing the high costs and technical complexity of scaling AI beyond pilot projects into full deployment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DOGE staffer allegedly ran company providing services to hacking group
item.description: US Department of Government Efficiency tech advisor Edward Coristine previously ran a small infrastructure provider that offered services to a cybercriminal group, it has been alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Secure encryption and online anonymity are now at risk in Switzerland – here's what you need to know
item.description: An amendment to the Swiss surveillance law would require VPNs and messaging apps to undermine their privacy and security framework. NymVPN, Proton, and Threema are fighting back.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Federal News Network’s CX Exchange 2025
item.description: End-to-End Experience – The evolution of federal service delivery The post Federal News Network’s CX Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach
item.description: Pete Waterman, the director of the cloud security program known as FedRAMP, said four new community working groups will help give industry a bigger voice. The post Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Samsung announces US prices for its 2025 mini-LED TV lineup, and it’s good and bad news
item.description: Samsung has announced US pricing for its new 4K and 8K mini-LED TVs, and prices for the flagship 8K series are largely higher than in 20204.
item.dc:creator: al.griffin@futurenet.com (Al Griffin), TechRadar

item.title: This watchdog is tracking how AI firms are quietly backing off their safety pledges
item.description: The companies racing to own the AI future have built their technologies and businesses by carefully keeping track of you and your data.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Snow, Fast Company

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SAP adoption surges in Europe as enterprises embrace cloud
item.description: Enterprises in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland are accelerating their transition to cloud-based ERP solutions, with SAP playing a key role in their digital transformation strategies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gee whiz, Gboard! An invaluable new Android typing upgrade
item.description: Ahh — don’t you just love the feeling of a fresh ‘n’ zesty virtual upgrade? Here in the land o’ Android, that doesn’t mean only major new Android versions (and, alas, the woeful waiting that often accompanies ’em).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Programming Languages on the Block: Go, Rust, and Zig
item.description: A look at three emerging programming languages in the software development space.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Styrolite: A New Open-Source Project for Container Security
item.description: Styrolite, a new open-source project from Edera, aims to bring tighter controls to container interactions and Linux kernel namespaces.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: The Senate confirmed Michael Kratsios to head the White House’s Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) with bipartisan support. Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: German startup Isar Aerospace cancels maiden voyage of orbital rocket
item.description: A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway. Unfavorable winds meant that the Spectrum rocket couldn’t be launched from the island of Andøya in northern Norway, Munich-based Isar Aerospace said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Striking the balance between information sharing and security
item.description: That balancing act requires a complex response, especially when designing networks and systems for collaboration. The post Striking the balance between information sharing and security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As federal employees return to office, agencies need a reliable, secure system to meet mandates and deliver efficient, productive facilities
item.description: Federal agencies need a unified platform that can deliver real-time reporting on occupancy, space availability, and opportunities for rightsizing.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: GenAI tools for R: New tools to make R programming easier
item.description: My previous article focused on some of the best tools for incorporating LLMs into your R scripts and workflows. We’ll expand on that theme here, offering up a collection of generative AI tools you can use to get help with your R programming or to run LLMs locally with R.
item.dc:creator: Info World
